NKJV.1: I commend to you Phoebe our sister, who is a servant of the church in Cenchrea,
NKJV.2: that you may receive her in the Lord in a manner worthy of the saints, and assist her in whatever business she has need of you; for indeed she has been a helper of many and of myself also.
NKJV.3: Greet Priscilla and Aquila, my fellow workers in Christ Jesus,
NKJV.4: who risked their own necks for my life, to whom not only I give thanks, but also all the churches of the Gentiles.
NKJV.5: Likewise greet the church that is in their house. Greet my beloved Epaenetus, who is the firstfruits of Achaia to Christ.
NKJV.6: Greet Mary, who labored much for us.
NKJV.7: Greet Andronicus and Junia, my countrymen and my fellow prisoners, who are of note among the apostles, who also were in Christ before me.
NKJV.8: Greet Amplias, my beloved in the Lord.
NKJV.9: Greet Urbanus, our fellow worker in Christ, and Stachys, my beloved.
NKJV.10: Greet Apelles, approved in Christ. Greet those who are of the household of Aristobulus.
NKJV.11: Greet Herodion, my countryman. Greet those who are of the household of Narcissus who are in the Lord.
NKJV.12: Greet Tryphena and Tryphosa, who have labored in the Lord. Greet the beloved Persis, who labored much in the Lord.
NKJV.13: Greet Rufus, chosen in the Lord, and his mother and mine.
NKJV.14: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermas, Patrobas, Hermes, and the brethren who are with them.
NKJV.15: Greet Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints who are with them.
NKJV.16: Greet one another with a holy kiss. The churches of Christ greet you.
NKJV.17: Now I urge you, brethren, note those who cause divisions and offenses, contrary to the doctrine which you learned, and avoid them.
NKJV.18: For those who are such do not serve our Lord Jesus Christ, but their own belly, and by smooth words and flattering speech deceive the hearts of the simple.
NKJV.19: For your obedience has become known to all. Therefore I am glad on your behalf; but I want you to be wise in what is good, and simple concerning evil.
NKJV.20: And the God of peace will crush Satan under your feet shortly. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you. Amen.
NKJV.21: Timothy, my fellow worker, and Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater, my countrymen, greet you.
NKJV.22: I, Tertius, who wrote this epistle, greet you in the Lord.
NKJV.23: Gaius, my host and the host of the whole church, greets you. Erastus, the treasurer of the city, greets you, and Quartus, a brother.
NKJV.24: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.
NKJV.25: Now to Him who is able to establish you according to my gospel and the preaching of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery kept secret since the world began
NKJV.26: but now made manifest, and by the prophetic Scriptures made known to all nations, according to the commandment of the everlasting God, for obedience to the faith—
NKJV.27: to God, alone wise, be glory through Jesus Christ forever. Amen.

ESV.1: I commend to you our sister Phoebe, a servant  of the church at Cenchreae,
ESV.2: that you may welcome her in the Lord in a way worthy of the saints, and help her in whatever she may need from you, for she has been a patron of many and of myself as well.
ESV.3: Greet Prisca and Aquila, my fellow workers in Christ Jesus,
ESV.4: who risked their necks for my life, to whom not only I give thanks but all the churches of the Gentiles give thanks as well.
ESV.5: Greet also the church in their house. Greet my beloved Epaenetus, who was the first convert  to Christ in Asia.
ESV.6: Greet Mary, who has worked hard for you.
ESV.7: Greet Andronicus and Junia,  my kinsmen and my fellow prisoners. They are well known to the apostles,  and they were in Christ before me.
ESV.8: Greet Ampliatus, my beloved in the Lord.
ESV.9: Greet Urbanus, our fellow worker in Christ, and my beloved Stachys.
ESV.10: Greet Apelles, who is approved in Christ. Greet those who belong to the family of Aristobulus.
ESV.11: Greet my kinsman Herodion. Greet those in the Lord who belong to the family of Narcissus.
ESV.12: Greet those workers in the Lord, Tryphaena and Tryphosa. Greet the beloved Persis, who has worked hard in the Lord.
ESV.13: Greet Rufus, chosen in the Lord; also his mother, who has been a mother to me as well.
ESV.14: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers  who are with them.
ESV.15: Greet Philologus, Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints who are with them.
ESV.16: Greet one another with a holy kiss. All the churches of Christ greet you.
ESV.17: I appeal to you, brothers, to watch out for those who cause divisions and create obstacles contrary to the doctrine that you have been taught; avoid them.
ESV.18: For such persons do not serve our Lord Christ, but their own appetites,  and by smooth talk and flattery they deceive the hearts of the naive.
ESV.19: For your obedience is known to all, so that I rejoice over you, but I want you to be wise as to what is good and innocent as to what is evil.
ESV.20: The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
ESV.21: Timothy, my fellow worker, greets you; so do Lucius and Jason and Sosipater, my kinsmen.
ESV.22: I Tertius, who wrote this letter, greet you in the Lord.
ESV.23: Gaius, who is host to me and to the whole church, greets you. Erastus, the city treasurer, and our brother Quartus, greet you.
ESV.25: Now to him who is able to strengthen you according to my gospel and the preaching of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery that was kept secret for long ages
ESV.26: but has now been disclosed and through the prophetic writings has been made known to all nations, according to the command of the eternal God, to bring about the obedience of faith —
ESV.27: to the only wise God be glory forevermore through Jesus Christ! Amen.

NASB.1: I commend to you our sister Phoebe, who is a servant of the church which is at Cenchrea;
NASB.2: that you receive her in the Lord in a manner worthy of the saints, and that you help her in whatever matter she may have need of you; for she herself has also been a helper of many, and of myself as well.
NASB.3: Greet Prisca and Aquila, my fellow workers in Christ Jesus,
NASB.4: who for my life risked their own necks, to whom not only do I give thanks, but also all the churches of the Gentiles;
NASB.5: also [greet] the church that is in their house. Greet Epaenetus, my beloved, who is the first convert to Christ from Asia.
NASB.6: Greet Mary, who has worked hard for you.
NASB.7: Greet Andronicus and Junias, my kinsmen and my fellow prisoners, who are outstanding among the apostles, who also were in Christ before me.
NASB.8: Greet Ampliatus, my beloved in the Lord.
NASB.9: Greet Urbanus, our fellow worker in Christ, and Stachys my beloved.
NASB.10: Greet Apelles, the approved in Christ. Greet those who are of the [household] of Aristobulus.
NASB.11: Greet Herodion, my kinsman. Greet those of the [household] of Narcissus, who are in the Lord.
NASB.12: Greet Tryphaena and Tryphosa, workers in the Lord. Greet Persis the beloved, who has worked hard in the Lord.
NASB.13: Greet Rufus, a choice man in the Lord, also his mother and mine.
NASB.14: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas and the brethren with them.
NASB.15: Greet Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints who are with them.
NASB.16: Greet one another with a holy kiss. All the churches of Christ greet you.
NASB.17: Now I urge you, brethren, keep your eye on those who cause dissensions and hindrances contrary to the teaching which you learned, and turn away from them.
NASB.18: For such men are slaves, not of our Lord Christ but of their own appetites; and by their smooth and flattering speech they deceive the hearts of the unsuspecting.
NASB.19: For the report of your obedience has reached to all; therefore I am rejoicing over you, but I want you to be wise in what is good and innocent in what is evil.
NASB.20: The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus be with you.
NASB.21: Timothy my fellow worker greets you, and [so] [do] Lucius and Jason and Sosipater, my kinsmen.
NASB.22: I, Tertius, who write this letter, greet you in the Lord.
NASB.23: Gaius, host to me and to the whole church, greets you. Erastus, the city treasurer greets you, and Quartus, the brother.
NASB.24: [The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen].
NASB.25: Now to Him who is able to establish you according to my gospel and the preaching of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery which has been kept secret for long ages past,
NASB.26: but now is manifested, and by the Scriptures of the prophets, according to the commandment of the eternal God, has been made known to all the nations, [leading] to obedience of faith;
NASB.27: to the only wise God, through Jesus Christ, be the glory forever. Amen.

NLT.1: I commend to you our sister Phoebe, who is a deacon in the church in Cenchrea.
NLT.2: Welcome her in the Lord as one who is worthy of honor among God’s people. Help her in whatever she needs, for she has been helpful to many, and especially to me.
NLT.3: Give my greetings to Priscilla and Aquila, my co-workers in the ministry of Christ Jesus.
NLT.4: In fact, they once risked their lives for me. I am thankful to them, and so are all the Gentile churches.
NLT.5: Also give my greetings to the church that meets in their home. Greet my dear friend Epenetus. He was the first person from the province of Asia to become a follower of Christ.
NLT.6: Give my greetings to Mary, who has worked so hard for your benefit.
NLT.7: Greet Andronicus and Junia, my fellow Jews, who were in prison with me. They are highly respected among the apostles and became followers of Christ before I did.
NLT.8: Greet Ampliatus, my dear friend in the Lord.
NLT.9: Greet Urbanus, our co-worker in Christ, and my dear friend Stachys.
NLT.10: Greet Apelles, a good man whom Christ approves. And give my greetings to the believers from the household of Aristobulus.
NLT.11: Greet Herodion, my fellow Jew. Greet the Lord’s people from the household of Narcissus.
NLT.12: Give my greetings to Tryphena and Tryphosa, the Lord’s workers, and to dear Persis, who has worked so hard for the Lord.
NLT.13: Greet Rufus, whom the Lord picked out to be his very own; and also his dear mother, who has been a mother to me.
NLT.14: Give my greetings to Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers and sisters who meet with them.
NLT.15: Give my greetings to Philologus, Julia, Nereus and his sister, and to Olympas and all the believers who meet with them.
NLT.16: Greet each other with a sacred kiss. All the churches of Christ send you their greetings.
NLT.17: And now I make one more appeal, my dear brothers and sisters. Watch out for people who cause divisions and upset people’s faith by teaching things contrary to what you have been taught. Stay away from them.
NLT.18: Such people are not serving Christ our Lord; they are serving their own personal interests. By smooth talk and glowing words they deceive innocent people.
NLT.19: But everyone knows that you are obedient to the Lord. This makes me very happy. I want you to be wise in doing right and to stay innocent of any wrong.
NLT.20: The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet. May the grace of our Lord Jesus be with you.
NLT.21: Timothy, my fellow worker, sends you his greetings, as do Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater, my fellow Jews.
NLT.22: I, Tertius, the one writing this letter for Paul, send my greetings, too, as one of the Lord’s followers.
NLT.23: Gaius says hello to you. He is my host and also serves as host to the whole church. Erastus, the city treasurer, sends you his greetings, and so does our brother Quartus.
NLT.25: Now all glory to God, who is able to make you strong, just as my Good News says. This message about Jesus Christ has revealed his plan for you Gentiles, a plan kept secret from the beginning of time.
NLT.26: But now as the prophets foretold and as the eternal God has commanded, this message is made known to all Gentiles everywhere, so that they too might believe and obey him.
NLT.27: All glory to the only wise God, through Jesus Christ, forever. Amen.

WEB.1: I commend to you Phoebe, our sister, who is a servant  of the assembly that is at Cenchreae,
WEB.2: that you receive her in the Lord, in a way worthy of the saints, and that you assist her in whatever matter she may need from you, for she herself also has been a helper of many, and of my own self.
WEB.3: Greet Prisca and Aquila, my fellow workers in Christ Jesus,
WEB.4: who for my life, laid down their own necks; to whom not only I give thanks, but also all the assemblies of the Gentiles.
WEB.5: Greet the assembly that is in their house. Greet Epaenetus, my beloved, who is the first fruits of Achaia to Christ.
WEB.6: Greet Mary, who labored much for us.
WEB.7: Greet Andronicus and Junia, my relatives and my fellow prisoners, who are notable among the apostles, who were also in Christ before me.
WEB.8: Greet Amplias, my beloved in the Lord.
WEB.9: Greet Urbanus, our fellow worker in Christ, and Stachys, my beloved.
WEB.10: Greet Apelles, the approved in Christ. Greet those who are of the household of Aristobulus.
WEB.11: Greet Herodion, my kinsman. Greet them of the household of Narcissus, who are in the Lord.
WEB.12: Greet Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who labor in the Lord. Greet Persis, the beloved, who labored much in the Lord.
WEB.13: Greet Rufus, the chosen in the Lord, and his mother and mine.
WEB.14: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers  who are with them.
WEB.15: Greet Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints who are with them.
WEB.16: Greet one another with a holy kiss. The assemblies of Christ greet you.
WEB.17: Now I beg you, brothers, look out for those who are causing the divisions and occasions of stumbling, contrary to the doctrine which you learned, and turn away from them.
WEB.18: For those who are such don’t serve our Lord, Jesus Christ, but their own belly; and by their smooth and flattering speech, they deceive the hearts of the innocent.
WEB.19: For your obedience has become known to all. I rejoice therefore over you. But I desire to have you wise in that which is good, but innocent in that which is evil.
WEB.20: And the God of peace will quickly crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
WEB.21: Timothy, my fellow worker, greets you, as do Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater, my relatives.
WEB.22: I, Tertius, who write the letter, greet you in the Lord.
WEB.23: Gaius, my host and host of the whole assembly, greets you. Erastus, the treasurer of the city, greets you, as does Quartus, the brother.
WEB.24: The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all! Amen.

CSB17.1: I commend to you <sup>ⓛ</sup> our sister Phoebe, who is a servant <sup>[1]</sup> of the church in Cenchreae. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.2: So you should welcome her in the Lord <sup>ⓝ</sup> in a manner worthy of the saints and assist her in whatever matter she may require your help. For indeed she has been a benefactor of many — and of me also.
CSB17.3: Give my greetings to Prisca <sup>[2]</sup> and Aquila, <sup>ⓐ</sup> my coworkers in <sup>ⓑ</sup> Christ Jesus, <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.4: who risked their own necks for my life. Not only do I thank them, but so do all the Gentile churches.
CSB17.5: Greet also the church that meets in their home. <sup>ⓓ</sup> Greet my dear friend Epaenetus, who is the first convert <sup>[3]</sup> <sup>ⓔ</sup> to Christ from Asia. <sup>[4]</sup> <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.6: Greet Mary, <sup>[5]</sup> who has worked very hard for you. <sup>[6]</sup>
CSB17.7: Greet Andronicus and Junia, my fellow Jews <sup>[7]</sup> <sup>ⓖ</sup> and fellow prisoners. <sup>ⓗ</sup> They are noteworthy in the eyes of the apostles, <sup>[8]</sup> and they were also in Christ before me.
CSB17.8: Greet Ampliatus, my dear friend in the Lord.
CSB17.9: Greet Urbanus, our coworker in Christ, and my dear friend Stachys.
CSB17.10: Greet Apelles, who is approved in Christ. Greet those who belong to the household of Aristobulus. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.11: Greet Herodion, my fellow Jew. <sup>[9]</sup> Greet those who belong to the household of Narcissus who are in the Lord.
CSB17.12: Greet Tryphaena and Tryphosa, who have worked hard in the Lord. Greet my dear friend Persis, who has worked very hard in the Lord.
CSB17.13: Greet Rufus, <sup>ⓙ</sup> chosen in the Lord; also his mother — and mine.
CSB17.14: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers and sisters who are with them.
CSB17.15: Greet Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints who are with them. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.16: Greet one another with a holy kiss. <sup>ⓛ</sup> All the churches of Christ send you greetings.
CSB17.17: Now I urge you, brothers and sisters, to watch out for those who create divisions and obstacles contrary to the teaching that you learned. <sup>ⓜ</sup> Avoid them, <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.18: because such people do not serve our Lord Christ <sup>ⓐ</sup> but their own appetites. <sup>[10]</sup> <sup>ⓑ</sup> They deceive the hearts of the unsuspecting with smooth talk and flattering words. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.19: The report of your obedience has reached everyone. <sup>ⓓ</sup> Therefore I rejoice over you, but I want you to be wise about what is good, and yet innocent about what is evil. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.20: The God of peace <sup>ⓕ</sup> will soon crush Satan <sup>ⓖ</sup> under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus be with you. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.21: Timothy, <sup>ⓘ</sup> my coworker, and Lucius, <sup>ⓙ</sup> Jason, <sup>ⓚ</sup> and Sosipater, <sup>ⓛ</sup> my fellow countrymen, greet you.
CSB17.22: I Tertius, who wrote this letter, <sup>ⓜ</sup> greet you in the Lord. <sup>[11]</sup>
CSB17.23: Gaius, <sup>ⓝ</sup> who is host to me and to the whole church, greets you. Erastus, <sup>ⓐ</sup> the city treasurer, and our brother Quartus greet you. <sup>[12]</sup>
CSB17.25: Now to him who is able to strengthen you <sup>ⓑ</sup> according to my gospel and the proclamation about Jesus Christ, <sup>ⓒ</sup> according to the revelation of the mystery kept silent <sup>ⓓ</sup> for long ages <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.26: but now revealed and made known through the prophetic Scriptures, <sup>ⓕ</sup> according to the command of the eternal God to advance the obedience of faith <sup>ⓖ</sup> among all the Gentiles —
CSB17.27: to the only wise God, through Jesus Christ — to him be the glory forever! <sup>[13]</sup> <sup>ⓗ</sup> Amen.

AMP.1: Now I introduce and commend to you our sister Phoebe, a deaconess (servant) of the church at Cenchrea,
AMP.2: that you may receive her in the Lord [with love and hospitality], as God's people ought to receive one another. And that you may help her in whatever matter she may require assistance from you, for she has been a helper of many, including myself.
AMP.3: Greet Prisca and Aquila, my fellow workers in Christ Jesus,
AMP.4: who risked their own necks [endangering their very lives] for my life. To them not only do I give thanks, but also all the churches of the Gentiles.
AMP.5: Also greet the church that meets in their house. Greet my beloved Epaenetus, who is the first convert to Christ from [the west coast province of] Asia [Minor].
AMP.6: Greet Mary, who has worked so hard for you.
AMP.7: Greet Andronicus and Junias, my kinsmen and [once] my fellow prisoners, who are held in high esteem in the estimation of the apostles, and who were [believers] in Christ before me.
AMP.8: Greet Ampliatus, my beloved in the Lord.
AMP.9: Greet Urbanus, our fellow worker in Christ, and my beloved Stachys.
AMP.10: Greet Apelles, the one tested and approved in Christ. Greet those who belong to the household of Aristobulus.
AMP.11: Greet my kinsman Herodion. Greet those of the household of Narcissus, who are in the Lord.
AMP.12: Greet those workers in the Lord, Tryphaena and Tryphosa. Greet my beloved Persis, who has worked hard in the Lord.
AMP.13: Greet Rufus, an eminent and choice man in the Lord, also his mother [who has been] a mother to me as well.
AMP.14: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers who are with them.
AMP.15: Greet Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all God's people who are with them.
AMP.16: Greet one another with a holy kiss. All the churches of Christ greet you.
AMP.17: I urge you, brothers and sisters, to keep your eyes on those who cause dissensions and create obstacles or introduce temptations [for others] to commit sin, [acting in ways] contrary to the doctrine which you have learned. Turn away from them.
AMP.18: For such people do not serve our Lord Christ, but their own appetites and base desires. By smooth and flattering speech they deceive the hearts of the unsuspecting [the innocent and the naive].
AMP.19: For the report of your obedience has reached everyone, so that I rejoice over you, but I want you to be wise in what is good and innocent in what is evil.
AMP.20:
The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet.
The [wonderful] grace of our Lord Jesus be with you.
AMP.21: Timothy, my fellow worker, sends his greetings to you, as do Lucius, Jason and Sosipater, my kinsmen.
AMP.22: I, Tertius, the writer (scribe) of this letter, greet you in the Lord.
AMP.23: Gaius, who is host to me and to the whole church here, greets you. Erastus, the city treasurer, and our brother Quartus, greet you.
AMP.24: [The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you all. Amen.]
AMP.25: Now to Him who is able to establish and strengthen you [in the faith] according to my gospel and the preaching of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery [of the plan of salvation] which has been kept secret for long ages past,
AMP.26: but now has been disclosed and through the prophetic Scriptures has been made known to all the nations, according to the commandment of the eternal God, leading them to obedience to the faith,
AMP.27: to the only wise God, through Jesus Christ, be the glory forevermore! Amen.

MSG.1: Be sure to welcome our friend Phoebe in the way of the Master, with all the generous hospitality we Christians are famous for. I heartily endorse both her and her work. She's a key representative of the church at Cenchrea.
MSG.2: Help her out in whatever she asks. She deserves anything you can do for her. She's helped many a person, including me.
MSG.3: Say hello to Priscilla and Aquila, who have worked hand in hand with me in serving Jesus.
MSG.4: They once put their lives on the line for me. And I'm not the only one grateful to them. All the non-Jewish gatherings of believers also owe them plenty,
MSG.5: to say nothing of the church that meets in their house. Hello to my dear friend Epenetus. He was the very first Christian in the province of Asia.
MSG.6: Hello to Mary. What a worker she has turned out to be!
MSG.7: Hello to my cousins Andronicus and Junias. We once shared a jail cell. They were believers in Christ before I was. Both of them are outstanding leaders.
MSG.8: Hello to Ampliatus, my good friend in the family of God.
MSG.9: Hello to Urbanus, our companion in Christ's work, and my good friend Stachys.
MSG.10: Hello to Apelles, a tried-and-true veteran in following Christ. Hello to the family of Aristobulus.
MSG.11: Hello to my cousin Herodion. Hello to those Christians from the family of Narcissus.
MSG.12: Hello to Tryphena and Tryphosa—such diligent women in serving the Master. Hello to Persis, a dear friend and hard worker in Christ.
MSG.13: Hello to Rufus—a good choice by the Master!—and his mother. She has also been a dear mother to me.
MSG.14: Hello to Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and also to all of their families.
MSG.15: Hello to Philologus, Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas—and all the Christians who live with them.
MSG.16: Holy embraces all around! All the churches of Christ send their warmest greetings!
MSG.17: One final word of counsel, friends. Keep a sharp eye out for those who take bits and pieces of the teaching that you learned and then use them to make trouble. Give these people a wide berth.
MSG.18: They have no intention of living for our Master Christ. They're only in this for what they can get out of it, and aren't above using pious sweet talk to dupe unsuspecting innocents.
MSG.19: And so while there has never been any question about your honesty in these matters—I couldn't be more proud of you!—I want you also to be smart, making sure every "good" thing is the real thing. Don't be gullible in regard to smooth-talking evil. Stay alert like this,
MSG.20: and before you know it the God of peace will come down on Satan with both feet, stomping him into the dirt. Enjoy the best of Jesus!
MSG.21: And here are some more greetings from our end. Timothy, my partner in this work, Lucius, and my cousins Jason and Sosipater all said to tell you hello.
MSG.22: I, Tertius, who wrote this letter at Paul's dictation, send you my personal greetings.
MSG.23: Gaius, who is host here to both me and the whole church, wants to be remembered to you. Erastus, the city treasurer, and our good friend Quartus send their greetings.
MSG.25: All of our praise rises to the One who is strong enough to make you strong, exactly as preached in Jesus Christ, precisely as revealed in the mystery kept secret for so long
MSG.26: but now an open book through the prophetic Scriptures. All the nations of the world can now know the truth and be brought into obedient belief, carrying out the orders of God, who got all this started, down to the very last letter.
MSG.27: All our praise is focused through Jesus on this incomparably wise God! Yes!

NIV.1:
Personal Greetings
I commend to you our sister Phoebe, a servant of the church in Cenchrea.
NIV.2: I ask you to receive her in the Lord in a way worthy of the saints and to give her any help she may need from you, for she has been a great help to many people, including me.
NIV.3: Greet Priscilla and Aquila, my fellow workers in Christ Jesus.
NIV.4: They risked their lives for me. Not only I but all the churches of the Gentiles are grateful to them.
NIV.5:
Greet also the church that meets at their house.
Greet my dear friend Epenetus, who was the first convert to Christ in the province of Asia.
NIV.6: Greet Mary, who worked very hard for you.
NIV.7: Greet Andronicus and Junias, my relatives who have been in prison with me. They are outstanding among the apostles, and they were in Christ before I was.
NIV.8: Greet Ampliatus, whom I love in the Lord.
NIV.9: Greet Urbanus, our fellow worker in Christ, and my dear friend Stachys.
NIV.10:
Greet Apelles, tested and approved in Christ.
Greet those who belong to the household of Aristobulus.
NIV.11:
Greet Herodion, my relative.
Greet those in the household of Narcissus who are in the Lord.
NIV.12:
Greet Tryphena and Tryphosa, those women who work hard in the Lord.
Greet my dear friend Persis, another woman who has worked very hard in the Lord.
NIV.13: Greet Rufus, chosen in the Lord, and his mother, who has been a mother to me, too.
NIV.14: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas and the brothers with them.
NIV.15: Greet Philologus, Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas and all the saints with them.
NIV.16:
Greet one another with a holy kiss.
All the churches of Christ send greetings.
NIV.17: I urge you, brothers, to watch out for those who cause divisions and put obstacles in your way that are contrary to the teaching you have learned. Keep away from them.
NIV.18: For such people are not serving our Lord Christ, but their own appetites. By smooth talk and flattery they deceive the minds of naive people.
NIV.19: Everyone has heard about your obedience, so I am full of joy over you; but I want you to be wise about what is good, and innocent about what is evil.
NIV.20:
The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet.
The grace of our Lord Jesus be with you.
NIV.21: Timothy, my fellow worker, sends his greetings to you, as do Lucius, Jason and Sosipater, my relatives.
NIV.22: I, Tertius, who wrote down this letter, greet you in the Lord.
NIV.23:
Gaius, whose hospitality I and the whole church here enjoy, sends you his greetings.
Erastus, who is the city’s director of public works, and our brother Quartus send you their greetings.
NIV.25: Now to him who is able to establish you by my gospel and the proclamation of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery hidden for long ages past,
NIV.26: but now revealed and made known through the prophetic writings by the command of the eternal God, so that all nations might believe and obey him—
NIV.27: to the only wise God be glory forever through Jesus Christ! Amen.

NRSVCE.1: I commend to you our sister Phoebe, a deacon of the church at Cenchreae,
NRSVCE.2: so that you may welcome her in the Lord as is fitting for the saints, and help her in whatever she may require from you, for she has been a benefactor of many and of myself as well.
NRSVCE.3: Greet Prisca and Aquila, who work with me in Christ Jesus,
NRSVCE.4: and who risked their necks for my life, to whom not only I give thanks, but also all the churches of the Gentiles.
NRSVCE.5: Greet also the church in their house. Greet my beloved Epaenetus, who was the first convert in Asia for Christ.
NRSVCE.6: Greet Mary, who has worked very hard among you.
NRSVCE.7: Greet Andronicus and Junia, my relatives who were in prison with me; they are prominent among the apostles, and they were in Christ before I was.
NRSVCE.8: Greet Ampliatus, my beloved in the Lord.
NRSVCE.9: Greet Urbanus, our co-worker in Christ, and my beloved Stachys.
NRSVCE.10: Greet Apelles, who is approved in Christ. Greet those who belong to the family of Aristobulus.
NRSVCE.11: Greet my relative Herodion. Greet those in the Lord who belong to the family of Narcissus.
NRSVCE.12: Greet those workers in the Lord, Tryphaena and Tryphosa. Greet the beloved Persis, who has worked hard in the Lord.
NRSVCE.13: Greet Rufus, chosen in the Lord; and greet his mother — a mother to me also.
NRSVCE.14: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers and sisters who are with them.
NRSVCE.15: Greet Philologus, Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the saints who are with them.
NRSVCE.16: Greet one another with a holy kiss. All the churches of Christ greet you.
NRSVCE.17: I urge you, brothers and sisters, to keep an eye on those who cause dissensions and offenses, in opposition to the teaching that you have learned; avoid them.
NRSVCE.18: For such people do not serve our Lord Christ, but their own appetites, and by smooth talk and flattery they deceive the hearts of the simple-minded.
NRSVCE.19: For while your obedience is known to all, so that I rejoice over you, I want you to be wise in what is good and guileless in what is evil.
NRSVCE.20: The God of peace will shortly crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with you.
NRSVCE.21: Timothy, my co-worker, greets you; so do Lucius and Jason and Sosipater, my relatives.
NRSVCE.22: I Tertius, the writer of this letter, greet you in the Lord.
NRSVCE.23: Gaius, who is host to me and to the whole church, greets you. Erastus, the city treasurer, and our brother Quartus, greet you.
NRSVCE.25: Now to God who is able to strengthen you according to my gospel and the proclamation of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery that was kept secret for long ages
NRSVCE.26: but is now disclosed, and through the prophetic writings is made known to all the Gentiles, according to the command of the eternal God, to bring about the obedience of faith —
NRSVCE.27: to the only wise God, through Jesus Christ, to whom be the glory forever! Amen.

NET.1: Now I commend to you our sister Phoebe, who is a servant of the church in Cenchrea,
NET.2: so that you may welcome her in the Lord in a way worthy of the saints and provide her with whatever help she may need from you, for she has been a great help to many, including me.
NET.3: Greet Prisca and Aquila, my fellow workers in Christ Jesus,
NET.4: who risked their own necks for my life. Not only I, but all the churches of the Gentiles are grateful to them.
NET.5: Also greet the church in their house. Greet my dear friend Epenetus, who was the first convert to Christ in the province of Asia.
NET.6: Greet Mary, who has worked very hard for you.
NET.7: Greet Andronicus and Junia, my compatriots and my fellow prisoners. They are well known to the apostles, and they were in Christ before me.
NET.8: Greet Ampliatus, my dear friend in the Lord.
NET.9: Greet Urbanus, our fellow worker in Christ, and my good friend Stachys.
NET.10: Greet Apelles, who is approved in Christ. Greet those who belong to the household of Aristobulus.
NET.11: Greet Herodion, my compatriot. Greet those in the household of Narcissus who are in the Lord.
NET.12: Greet Tryphena and Tryphosa, laborers in the Lord. Greet my dear friend Persis, who has worked hard in the Lord.
NET.13: Greet Rufus, chosen in the Lord, and his mother who was also a mother to me.
NET.14: Greet Asyncritus, Phlegon, Hermes, Patrobas, Hermas, and the brothers and sisters with them.
NET.15: Greet Philologus and Julia, Nereus and his sister, and Olympas, and all the believers who are with them.
NET.16: Greet one another with a holy kiss. All the churches of Christ greet you.
NET.17: Now I urge you, brothers and sisters, to watch out for those who create dissensions and obstacles contrary to the teaching that you learned. Avoid them!
NET.18: For these are the kind who do not serve our Lord Christ, but their own appetites. By their smooth talk and flattery they deceive the minds of the naive.
NET.19: Your obedience is known to all and thus I rejoice over you. But I want you to be wise in what is good and innocent in what is evil.
NET.20: The God of peace will quickly crush Satan under your feet. The grace of our Lord Jesus be with you.
NET.21: Timothy, my fellow worker, greets you; so do Lucius, Jason, and Sosipater, my compatriots.
NET.22: I, Tertius, who am writing this letter, greet you in the Lord.
NET.23: Gaius, who is host to me and to the whole church, greets you. Erastus the city treasurer and our brother Quartus greet you.
NET.25: Now to him who is able to strengthen you according to my gospel and the proclamation of Jesus Christ, according to the revelation of the mystery that had been kept secret for long ages,
NET.26: but now is disclosed, and through the prophetic scriptures has been made known to all the nations, according to the command of the eternal God, to bring about the obedience of faith —
NET.27: to the only wise God, through Jesus Christ, be glory forever! Amen.
